homepage.heroSubline: Summit Auto Repair delivers reliable service for your vehicle. We treat every car like it's our own.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Summit Auto Repair started as a small garage in East Memphis. Over the years, we've grown by focusing on honest diagnostics and fair pricing.
Our technicians use manufacturer-recommended procedures, not shortcuts. We explain repairs in plain terms so you can make informed decisions.
As Memphis natives, we understand how Mississippi River humidity and summer heat affect vehicles. We're proud to support local schools and youth sports teams.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Diagnostics
valueProps.description: Get a clear assessment of your vehicle's issues without waiting days for answers.

valueProps.title: Local Parts Network
valueProps.description: We source quality parts from trusted Memphis suppliers to keep costs down.

valueProps.title: Loaner Cars Available
valueProps.description: Borrow a vehicle while we work on yours for no extra charge on major repairs.

valueProps.title: 12-Month Warranties
valueProps.description: All repairs come with a warranty because we stand behind our work.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-AUT-R91 or stop by our shop on Summer Avenue.

services.name: Brake Repair
services.shortDescription: Complete brake inspections and repairs to keep you safe on Memphis roads.
services.description:
Memphis' stop-and-go traffic on Poplar Avenue and I-240 wears down brake components faster than highway driving. We test rotors for warping from heat buildup, check caliper function, and measure pad thickness.

Our brake service includes resurfacing or replacing rotors as needed, installing quality pads, and flushing old fluid. We adjust parking brakes properly for Memphis' hilly neighborhoods like Harbor Town.

After repairs, we road test vehicles on routes with steep grades near the Mississippi River to ensure proper stopping power.
process: Step 1: Measure pad thickness and rotor condition
Step 2: Test brake fluid moisture content
Step 3: Remove wheels and inspect calipers
Step 4: Replace components and bleed system

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers replace brake pads?
faq.answer: Every 30,000-45,000 miles depending on driving habits. Downtown commuters need more frequent changes than suburban drivers.

faq.question: Why do my brakes squeak after rain?
faq.answer: Morning humidity near the river causes temporary surface rust on rotors. Persistent noise indicates worn components.

services.name: Engine Diagnostics
services.shortDescription: Advanced computer scans to pinpoint engine issues accurately.
services.description:
Modern vehicles have complex computer systems that require specialized tools to diagnose. We use OEM-grade scanners to read trouble codes from your vehicle's ECU, not just generic code readers.

Memphis' summer heat stresses cooling systems and can trigger false sensor readings. We perform live data monitoring during test drives to see how systems behave in real conditions.

For intermittent issues common in older Memphis vehicles, we can install data loggers to track problems that don't trigger check engine lights. This helps diagnose electrical gremlins in historic neighborhoods where wiring ages faster.
process: Step 1: Connect to OBD-II port
Step 2: Retrieve stored and pending codes
Step 3: Monitor live sensor data
Step 4: Perform component tests

faq.question: Why does my check engine light come on in Memphis summers?
faq.answer: Heat causes fuel vapor issues in older vehicles. We check evaporative systems for leaks that trigger false emissions codes.

faq.question: Can you diagnose transmission problems too?
faq.Answer: Yes, our scanners access transmission control modules to identify shift solenoid or clutch pack issues.

services.name: AC Repair
services.shortDescription: Cooling system services for Memphis' humid summers.
services.description:
Driving without AC in Memphis isn't just uncomfortable—it's dangerous during heat advisories. We pressure test systems to find leaks in rubber hoses that dry out in our climate.

Our AC service includes evacuating old refrigerant (properly disposed of), replacing receiver-driers clogged with moisture, and recharging with exact manufacturer specifications. We test compressor function under load to catch weak components before they fail.

For classic cars stored in Memphis garages, we offer R-12 to R-134a conversions that work better in stoplight traffic. We also install UV dye to make future leak detection easier.
process: Step 1: Pressure test system
Step 2: Identify leaks with electronic detector
Step 3: Replace failed components
Step 4: Recharge with proper refrigerant

faq.question: Why does my AC work sometimes in Memphis?
faq.Answer: Intermittent cooling usually indicates a failing clutch or low refrigerant causing the system to cut out.

faq.question: How often should AC systems be serviced?
faq.Answer: Every 2 years to maintain efficiency. Older systems may need annual checks.

services.name: Transmission Service
services.shortDescription: Fluid changes and adjustments for smooth shifting.
services.description:
Memphis drivers put extra stress on transmissions with constant acceleration from traffic lights. We perform drain-and-fill services that replace more fluid than quick lube places.

For vehicles with over 100,000 miles, we install auxiliary transmission coolers to handle summer heat. Our service includes adjusting bands on older vehicles and resetting adaptive shift computers on newer models.

We test transmission function on hills near the Wolf River to ensure proper gear engagement. For problematic transmissions, we partner with local rebuilders instead of pushing unnecessary replacements.
process: Step 1: Drain old fluid and inspect
Step 2: Replace filter if accessible
Step 3: Refill with correct fluid type
Step 4: Road test under load

faq.question: How often should I change transmission fluid?
faq.Answer: Every 30,000 miles for most vehicles. Heavy city driving may require more frequent changes.

faq.question: Why does my transmission slip when it's hot outside?
faq.Answer: Thinning fluid from heat causes pressure loss. We test line pressure to diagnose internal wear.

services.name: Suspension Repair
services.shortDescription: Restoring ride quality on Memphis' uneven roads.
services.description:
Potholes on Union Avenue and winter freeze-thaw cycles wreck suspension components. We inspect ball joints, control arms, and struts for play that causes uneven tire wear.

Our alignment service includes adjusting camber and toe to factory specs, then test driving on roads with crowned surfaces like Park Avenue. We check steering components for looseness that makes highway driving tiring.

For trucks used on Memphis construction sites, we upgrade to heavy-duty shocks. We also install protective boots on tie rods to prevent dirt intrusion from unpaved lots.
process: Step 1: Lift vehicle and check for play
Step 2: Measure alignment angles
Step 3: Replace worn components
Step 4: Road test on varied surfaces

faq.question: Why does my car pull after hitting a Memphis pothole?
faq.Answer: Impact likely bent a control arm or knocked alignment out of spec. We check for hidden damage.

faq.question: How long do struts typically last here?
faq.Answer: About 70,000 miles with our road conditions. Leaking fluid or bottoming out means replacement is due.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: They replaced my warped rotors and installed ceramic pads. My truck finally stops smoothly when coming down the I-40 ramp at Danny Thomas.

reviews.name: Latoya M.
reviews.text: Found the leak in my compressor seal that three other shops missed. Now my AC blows cold even sitting in traffic on Elvis Presley Blvd.

reviews.name: Robert K.
reviews.text: Adjusted the bands on my old Silverado and it shifts like new again. Saved me from needing a rebuild right before summer.

reviews.name: Susan P.
reviews.text: They diagnosed my random stalling issue as a bad crank sensor. No more getting stranded at Shelby Farms.

reviews.name: Marcus D.
reviews.text: Replaced my worn out struts and aligned it perfectly. Car handles the curves on Riverside Drive like it's on rails now.

reviews.name: Elena R.
reviews.text: Fixed my electrical issue causing the AC to cut out. Their loaner car got me through the heat wave while they worked on it.

Our convenient location makes it easy for Memphis residents to access professional auto care without the hassle.
